title: veridict power --sprt report
description: JSON output of `veridict power --sprt`. Estimates the expected number of decisive trials to a Wald SPRT decision under each hypothesis (Wald's classical Average Sample Number approximation), given elo0/elo1/alpha/beta - the same inputs `veridict sprt --sprt-variant wald` itself takes. Structurally distinct from power-report.schema.json (the CI-crossing-probability mode): Wald's alpha/beta already fix the guaranteed error rates, so there is no target_power to search a sample size for.

schema_version.description: Stays 1 across additive changes; bumps only when a field is removed or renamed.

elo0.description: H0: the candidate is at most this many logistic-Elo points stronger. Same meaning as `veridict sprt --elo0`.

elo1.description: H1: the candidate is at least this many logistic-Elo points stronger. Must be strictly greater than elo0.

alpha.description: False-positive rate: probability of accepting H1 when H0 is true.

beta.description: False-negative rate: probability of accepting H0 when H1 is true.

expected_trials_under_h0.description: Wald's ASN approximation for the expected number of decisive trials to a decision, assuming the true model sits exactly at H0.

expected_trials_under_h1.description: Wald's ASN approximation for the expected number of decisive trials to a decision, assuming the true model sits exactly at H1.

horizon.description: Echoes --horizon when given; omitted entirely (not null) otherwise.

probability_no_decision_by_horizon.description: Monte Carlo estimate (2,000 replications, fixed seed) of P(no decision within `horizon` decisive trials), evaluated at the true strength exactly halfway between elo0/elo1 - the realistic worst case per expected_trials_under_h0/h1's own doc, not either endpoint. Present iff horizon is.

notes.description: Always includes the overshoot-approximation caveat (a real run typically needs somewhat more trials than this number - measured empirically in tests/calibration/sprt_asn_calibration.rs) and the decisive-trials-only/draws caveat. An additional note is appended when horizon is given, explaining probability_no_decision_by_horizon's Monte Carlo method and that it doesn't change how a real run should be stopped.
